action.reasoning: Observation: the fixed ViT-L frozen-feature + domain-adversarial head produced a major jump to 0.3894 macro-F1, clearly the best result so far, but training curves show rising train accuracy with flat/noisy val accuracy and high val loss, suggesting overfitting/calibration issues. Analysis: with only ~3 hours left, the safest high-upside move is not a radical architecture change but a stronger inference/training regularization pass on the same successful pipeline. The most promising cheap gain is multi-view test-time ensembling beyond horizontal flip, plus label smoothing and EMA-like checkpoint selection via best val macro-F1. Since features are frozen, adding 5-crop style deterministic views is feasible at prediction time and can improve OOD robustness. To keep iteration risk low, I will preserve the current DG setup, add configurable multi-view feature extraction (center + flip + resized variants), add label smoothing for class loss, and train a moderate run. Then I will smoke-test predict.py locally and submit eval immediately.
action.command: python train.py --data_dir /data --model_name openai/clip-vit-large-patch14 --img_size 224 --batch_size 64 --num_workers 8 --epochs 8 --lr 0.0015 --weight_decay 0.0002 --hidden_dim 1024 --dropout 0.4 --use_flip --domain_adv --domain_loss_weight 0.1 --grl_lambda 0.4 --label_smoothing 0.12 --save_path checkpoints/best.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/best.pt --output val_preds.json
